hero.eyebrow: United Empire of Earth — Org Commerce
hero.subtitle: For Star Citizen Organizations
hero.desc: Real-time inventory management, Discord-native buy/sell workflows, CSV bulk import with SC Wiki auto-lookup, and live dashboards — all in one HUD built for serious orgs.

howItWorks.label: How it works
howItWorks.step1Desc: A member initiates a buy or sell via the web terminal or Discord slash commands — with inventory autocomplete.
howItWorks.step2Desc: An admin approves or rejects — directly in the terminal or by clicking a button on the Discord embed.
howItWorks.step3Desc: Both parties complete the exchange inside Star Citizen, then each confirms it in the terminal or via Discord.
howItWorks.step4Desc: Once both sides confirm, the transaction closes and inventory stock adjusts automatically. Charts update in real time.

features.label: Features
features.dashboardDesc: Real-time KPI cards, 30-day revenue and volume charts, top-performing items, and a live activity feed — all updated the moment any transaction changes state.
features.inventoryDesc: Full catalogue of org goods with auto stock sync. Import your entire item catalogue in bulk via CSV — metadata fetched automatically from the SC Wiki. Stock adjusts as trades complete.
features.workflowsDesc: Full multi-step lifecycle: request → approve → in-game confirm → complete. Either party can initiate, both must confirm before stock moves.
features.discordDesc: Members use /sell and /buy with inventory autocomplete. Every transaction posts a rich embed with live action buttons — approve, confirm, cancel — without leaving Discord.
features.membersDesc: Full member lifecycle management with four roles (Owner / Admin / HR / Member), custom rank system, member profiles with notes and tags, suspend/reactivate, bulk rank assignment, invite via Discord DM, and a detail drawer showing role and rank history.
features.auditDesc: Every action — transaction changes, inventory edits, role changes, settings updates — is recorded with timestamp and actor. Full history for org owners.
features.notificationsDesc: Instant in-app alerts for every transaction update, invitation, and org event. Live bell icon in the terminal header with unread badge, notification inbox, and one-click mark-as-read.
features.csvImportDesc: Populate your entire inventory in one go. Upload a spreadsheet of item names with optional pricing and stock values — each name is looked up against the Star Citizen Wiki to pull in category, class, grade, and size automatically. Async processing with live progress tracking, per-row result reporting, and a downloadable retry file for any misses.
features.auecDesc: Exchange real aUEC between the org and its members. Org admins set the balance and buy/sell rates; members submit requests that go through the standard approve-and-confirm workflow.
features.inviteDesc: Share a single permanent link to let anyone join your org. No expiry, no per-user tokens — regenerate or revoke it any time from Settings.
features.reportingDesc: Auto-generated PDF reports every Monday covering every completed trade — KPI summary, credit flow, top items, most active members, inventory movement, and week-over-week trends. Download or regenerate any past week on demand.

discordSection.label: Discord Integration
discordSection.title: Never leave Discord
discordSection.desc: The bot integrates fully into your org's workflow. Members create trade requests, admins approve them, and both sides confirm the in-game exchange — all through interactive Discord embeds that update in real time.
discordSection.sellDesc: Sell goods to the org — picks items from your inventory with live autocomplete
discordSection.buyDesc: Request to purchase from the org stockpile — same autocomplete experience
discordSection.footnote: ↳ One Discord server per org · Notification channel configurable in Settings

dashboardSection.label: Live Dashboard
dashboardSection.title: Real-time org overview
dashboardSection.desc: The dashboard connects live to the server. The moment any transaction changes state — anywhere, by anyone — you receive a toast notification and the dashboard silently refreshes. No manual refresh, no polling.
dashboardSection.li1: KPI cards: active requests, completed trades, revenue, item count
dashboardSection.li2: 30-day revenue area chart and sell/buy volume bar chart
dashboardSection.li3: Top 5 items by revenue with percentage bars
dashboardSection.li4: Last 10 completed trades at a glance
dashboardSection.li5: Live activity feed with real-time status updates

cta.title: Get your org online
cta.desc: Log in with Discord, create an organization, connect the bot to your server, and start managing trades in minutes.

footer.disclaimer: Unauthorized access is a violation of UEE code §18.7 — v0.1.0

pricing.label: Pricing
pricing.freeDesc: Everything you need to run a serious trading org — no credit card required.
pricing.proNote: Cancel anytime · One subscription per organisation
pricing.proFeature2: CSV Bulk Import — up to 500 items
pricing.proFeature4: Google Sheets Sync — auto every 10 min
pricing.proFeature5: Weekly Reports — auto PDF every Monday
pricing.proFeature6: Exclusive Discord Role — PRO role, exclusive channels & faster support
pricing.proFeature7: Member CSV Export — full roster download (PRO)

csvImport.title: Bulk CSV Import
csvImport.description: Import multiple items at once from a CSV file. Item metadata is fetched from the Star Citizen Wiki automatically.
csvImport.dropzone: Drag & drop your CSV here, or
csvImport.historyDescription: Browse all past CSV import jobs for this organization.
csvImport.historyNoImports: No imports have been run yet.

faq.title: Help & FAQ
faq.description: Everything you need to know about the SC Orga terminal.

dashboard.title: Dashboard
dashboard.subtitle: Real-time org overview
dashboard.q1Intro: The dashboard is your command center. It displays four KPI cards at a glance:
dashboard.q1Li1: <h>Active Requests</h> — transactions currently in Requested or Approved state.
dashboard.q1Li2: <h>Completed this month</h> — trades that reached Completed status since the 1st of the current month.
dashboard.q1Li3: <h>Revenue this month</h> — total aUEC from completed trades this month.
dashboard.q1Li4: <h>Inventory items</h> — how many item types are listed in the org store.
dashboard.q1Outro: Below the KPIs you will find two charts (revenue curve and transaction volume bars), a top-items leaderboard, the last 10 completed trades, and platform news & updates.
dashboard.q2: How does the real-time feed work?
dashboard.q2Intro: The dashboard opens a persistent connection (SSE — Server-Sent Events). Every 3 seconds the server checks for updates. When a status change is detected:
dashboard.q2Li1: A <h>toast notification</h> appears in the bottom-right corner (auto-dismisses after 6 s, max 5 at once).
dashboard.q2Li2: A new entry is added to the <h>Live Activity Feed</h> (keeps last 30 events).
dashboard.q2Li3: All KPI cards, charts, and recent trades refresh silently in the background.
dashboard.q2Outro: The feed indicator shows <h>● Live</h> once the connection is established.

inventorySection.title: Inventory
inventorySection.subtitle: Managing your org's item catalogue
inventorySection.q1Body: An inventory item represents a Star Citizen item or commodity your org buys or sells. Each entry tracks the item name (sourced from the SC Wiki), current stock quantity, buy price, sell price in aUEC, and optional min/max stock thresholds.
inventorySection.q2: How do I add items to inventory?
inventorySection.q2Intro: Open the <h>Inventory</h> page and expand the <c>+ Add Item</c> accordion. Start typing — the autocomplete searches the SC Wiki. Enable <c>Commodities only</c> to filter for tradeable cargo items. Select one or more variants, enter quantity, buy/sell prices, and optionally min/max stock thresholds.
inventorySection.q2Admin: Only <h>admins</h> and <h>owners</h> can add or remove inventory items.
inventorySection.q3Intro: Stock is automatically adjusted when a transaction is completed:
inventorySection.q3Li1: <h>Sell (member → org)</h> — member delivers goods to the org; org stock increases.
inventorySection.q3Li2: <h>Buy (org → member)</h> — member receives goods from the org; org stock decreases.

cargoSection.title: Cargo Tab
cargoSection.subtitle: Bulk commodity trading in SCU
cargoSection.q1Body: The Cargo Tab is a dedicated section for trading bulk commodities measured in SCU (Standard Cargo Units). Unlike inventory items which are counted by unit, cargo entries track quantities in SCU. Access it via the tab navigation on the Inventory page.
cargoSection.q2: How do I add cargo items?
cargoSection.q2Intro: Admins and owners can add cargo items from the Cargo Tab. The autocomplete defaults to <c>Commodities only</c> mode, showing only cargo-compatible items from the SC Wiki.
cargoSection.q2Pricing: Cargo pricing is expressed as <h>price per SCU</h>. The total aUEC for a transaction is calculated as <c>price per SCU × quantity SCU</c>.
cargoSection.q3: How does a cargo transaction work?
cargoSection.q3Body: Cargo transactions follow the same approve → confirm flow as inventory transactions. When completed, the cargo item's stock (in SCU) is adjusted automatically and the org aUEC balance is updated accordingly.

auecSection.title: aUEC Cash Desk
auecSection.subtitle: Direct aUEC exchange between members and the org
auecSection.q1: What is the aUEC Cash Desk?
auecSection.q1Body: The aUEC Cash Desk is a tab on the Inventory page that lets members exchange aUEC directly with the organisation. Members can deposit aUEC into the org pool or withdraw from it, subject to the org's available aUEC balance.
auecSection.q2: How do I submit an aUEC transaction?
auecSection.q2Intro: Go to the <h>Inventory</h> page and select the <h>aUEC</h> tab. Choose a direction (deposit or withdraw), enter the amount, and submit. The flow follows the same dual-confirm pattern:
auecSection.q2Li3: Both parties confirm the in-game aUEC transfer.
auecSection.q2Li4: On completion, the org aUEC balance and the member's personal aUEC balance are both adjusted automatically.
auecSection.q3: Where can I see my aUEC balance?
auecSection.q3Body: Your personal aUEC balance is shown in the <h>Members</h> list and on the <h>Account Settings</h> page. Admins can view and adjust member balances from the Members page.

proSection.title: PRO Plan
proSection.subtitle: Premium features and billing
proSection.q1Body: The PRO plan is a paid subscription that unlocks advanced features for your organisation. It is managed per organisation via Stripe — each org upgrades independently. PRO features are hidden for Free plan orgs.
proSection.q2Intro: The following features are exclusively available on the PRO plan:
proSection.q2Li1: <h>Bulk CSV Import</h> — import hundreds of inventory items at once from a spreadsheet.
proSection.q2Li2: <h>CSV Export</h> — export your current inventory to a CSV file.
proSection.q2Li3: <h>Google Sheets Sync</h> — automatically push your inventory to a Google Sheet whenever it changes.
proSection.q2Li4: <h>Weekly Reports</h> — auto-generated PDF report every Monday.
proSection.q2Li5: <h>Exclusive Discord Role</h> — get the PRO role on our Discord server for exclusive channels and faster support.
proSection.q3: How do I upgrade to PRO?
proSection.q3Body: Go to <h>Settings</h> and find the <h>Billing</h> card at the bottom. Click <c>Upgrade to PRO</c>. You will be redirected to Stripe Checkout to enter your payment details. After payment you are returned to the Settings page with PRO instantly active.
proSection.q4: How do I manage my subscription or view invoices?
proSection.q4Body: From <h>Settings</h>, click <c>Invoices & Manage</c> in the Billing card. This opens a page showing your subscription status, all past invoices (downloadable as PDF), and a <c>Cancel Plan</c> button. Cancellation is soft — you keep PRO access until the end of the current billing period.
proSection.q5: How does Google Sheets Sync work?
proSection.q5Intro: Google Sheets Sync automatically pushes your inventory to a Google Sheet whenever it changes. Setup:
proSection.q5Step1: Create a Google Sheet and share it (edit access) with the service account email shown in <h>Settings</h>.
proSection.q5Step2: Copy the Google Sheet URL and paste it into the <c>Google Sheets</c> card in Settings, then click <c>Save</c>.
proSection.q5Step3: Click <c>Sync Now</c> to trigger an immediate push, or wait for automatic sync on the next inventory change.
proSection.q5Outro: The sheet is overwritten on each sync with current item names, categories, stock, and prices.
proSection.q6: How does Bulk CSV Import work?
proSection.q6Intro: Admins can bulk-import inventory items from a CSV file. Only the <c>name</c> column is required:
proSection.q6Step1: Expand the <c>Bulk CSV Import</c> accordion on the Inventory page.
proSection.q6Step2: Download the CSV template, fill in item names and optional pricing / stock columns, then upload.
proSection.q6Step3: Preview the parsed rows and click Start Import. Processing runs in the background.
proSection.q6Step4: When finished, you receive an in-app notification and a Discord DM with a summary.
proSection.q6Outro: Optional columns: buyPrice, sellPrice, quantity, minStock, maxStock. Max 500 rows per import. Failed rows can be downloaded as a CSV for correction.
proSection.q7: Can I export the member roster?
proSection.q7Body: Yes — PRO orgs can download a <h>CSV export</h> of all members from the Members tab. The export includes username, display name, role, rank, status, tags, aUEC balance, and join date. Click the <h>Export CSV</h> button in the toolbar on the Members tab. Free orgs see the button greyed out with a PRO badge.

reportingSection.title: Weekly Reporting
reportingSection.subtitle: PRO — automated PDF reports
reportingSection.q1Body: Weekly Reporting is a PRO feature that automatically generates a PDF report every Monday for the previous week. It summarises all completed trades, KPI metrics, credit flow, top traded items, most active members, and inventory movement — with week-over-week trend comparison.
reportingSection.q2Li1: <h>Transaction log</h> — every completed trade that week (up to 500 rows; truncated with a warning if exceeded).
reportingSection.q2Li2: <h>KPI summary</h> — total transactions, credits earned/spent, net credit flow, and org aUEC pool delta.
reportingSection.q2Li3: <h>Top traded items</h> — top 5 items by volume with quantity, trade count, and net credits.
reportingSection.q2Li4: <h>Most active members</h> — top 5 members by trade count.
reportingSection.q2Li5: <h>Largest trade</h> — the single highest-value transaction of the week.
reportingSection.q2Li6: <h>Week-over-week trends</h> — delta vs. the previous week's report (if available).
reportingSection.q3Body: Reports are generated <h>automatically every Monday at 02:00 UTC</h> for all PRO orgs covering the previous ISO week (Monday–Sunday). Admins and owners can also generate or regenerate any week <h>on demand</h> from the Reporting page.
reportingSection.q4: How do I access the Reporting page?
reportingSection.q4Intro: The Reporting page is accessible from the sidebar navigation (PRO orgs only):
reportingSection.q4Step1: Go to your org and click <c>Reporting</c> in the left navigation sidebar (visible only for PRO orgs to admins and owners).
reportingSection.q4Step2: Click <c>Generate Report</c> to create a report for the current week, or wait for the Monday auto-generation.
reportingSection.q4Step3: Once the status shows Ready, click Download to save the PDF.
reportingSection.q5: Can I regenerate a report after transactions are edited?
reportingSection.q5Body: Yes. Admins and owners can click <c>Regenerate</c> on any existing report. This recalculates all KPIs and rebuilds the PDF from current transaction data, incrementing the version number. The previous file is overwritten.
reportingSection.q6: What happens if report generation fails?
reportingSection.q6Body: The report status changes to Failed and an error message is shown on the Reporting page. The scheduler will automatically retry up to 3 times on subsequent Monday runs. After 3 failures, all org admins and owners receive an in-app notification to regenerate manually.

transactionsSection.title: Transactions
transactionsSection.subtitle: The buy/sell request flow
transactionsSection.q1: What are the two transaction directions?
transactionsSection.q1Li1: <h>Sell (member → org)</h> — the member has goods and wants to sell them to the organization. The org pays the member.
transactionsSection.q1Li2: <h>Buy (org → member)</h> — the member wants to buy goods from the organization's stock. The member pays the org.
transactionsSection.q2: What are all the transaction statuses?
transactionsSection.q2Li1: — Initial state. One side has submitted the request and is waiting for the other to approve it.
transactionsSection.q2Li2: — The counterparty accepted the trade. Both parties now need to confirm the in-game transfer.
transactionsSection.q2Li3: — Both sides confirmed the in-game trade. Inventory stock is adjusted automatically.
transactionsSection.q3: Who can approve, confirm, and cancel?
transactionsSection.q3Intro: The approval rules depend on who initiated the request:
transactionsSection.q3Li1: If a <h>member</h> created the request, an <h>admin/owner</h> must approve it.
transactionsSection.q3Li2: If an <h>admin/owner</h> created the request, the <h>member</h> must approve it.
transactionsSection.q3Li3: Once Approved, both parties independently click <c>Confirm Trade</c> to record the in-game exchange.
transactionsSection.q3Li4: Either party can <c>Cancel</c> at any point before completion.
transactionsSection.q4: Can I create transactions from Discord?
transactionsSection.q4Body: Yes — if the Discord bot is connected. Use the <c>/sell</c> or <c>/buy</c> slash commands. See the <h>Discord Bot</h> section for full details.

membersSection.title: Members & Invites
membersSection.subtitle: Roles, ranks, profiles and invitations
membersSection.q1Li1: <h>Owner</h> — full access: audit logs, org deletion, billing, rank management, and all member actions. One owner per org.
membersSection.q1Li2: <h>Admin</h> — inventory, transactions, invite at any role, manage member roles/suspension/removal, access settings and billing.
membersSection.q1Li3: <h>HR</h> — Members tab only: assign ranks, edit profiles (display name, notes, tags), and invite new members at Member role. Cannot access settings, billing, or transactions.
membersSection.q1Li4: <h>Member</h> — can create transaction requests and confirm their own trades.
membersSection.q2Body: The <h>HR</h> role is designed for members who manage the roster but should not have access to financial or configuration data. HR users can see the Members tab, assign ranks, edit member profiles (display name, internal notes, tags), and send Discord invites — but only for the Member role. They cannot access Settings, Billing, Audit Logs, or Transactions.
membersSection.q3Body: Owners and admins can create a custom rank hierarchy under the <h>Ranks</h> tab on the Members page. Each rank has a name, description, sort order (1 = lowest), and a badge colour. One rank can be set as <h>Default</h> and will be auto-assigned to every new member. Ranks are displayed as coloured badges throughout the app. You can reorder ranks by dragging the rows.
membersSection.q4: What does the member detail drawer show?
membersSection.q4Body: Click any member row to open the detail drawer. From there you can edit the member's display name, internal notes, and tags; assign or change their rank; change their role; and see a chronological history of every role change and rank assignment. Owners and admins can also suspend, reactivate, or remove the member from this panel.
membersSection.q5: How do I invite someone via Discord?
membersSection.q5Body: Go to <h>Members then Invitations</h> and click <h>New Invite</h>. The Discord bot must be connected first (see Settings). Type a Discord username to search, choose a target role (HR users are limited to Member), set an optional expiry date, and send. The bot DMs the user a private single-use invite link.
membersSection.q6: What is the permanent invite link?
membersSection.q6Body: Admins and owners can generate a <h>permanent invite link</h> from the Settings page. This link can be shared freely (e.g. pinned in your Discord server) and stays valid until explicitly revoked. Each click joins the user as a Member. Only one permanent link is active at a time — generating a new one revokes the previous one.
membersSection.q7: Can I suspend or remove a member?
membersSection.q7Body: Yes — owners and admins can <h>suspend</h> a member from the detail drawer. A suspended member loses access to the org immediately on their next page load and is redirected to the terminal home. They remain in the member list (marked Suspended) and can be reactivated at any time. Removing a member is permanent and deletes them from the org entirely.

discordBot.title: Discord Bot
discordBot.subtitle: Setup, slash commands, and interactive embeds
discordBot.q1: How do I add the Discord bot to my server?
discordBot.q1Step2: In the <h>Discord Server</h> card at the top, click <c>Add Bot to Server</c>. You will be redirected to Discord's OAuth flow.
discordBot.q1Step3: Select the server you want to link to this organization and click <c>Authorize</c>.
discordBot.q1Step4: You will be redirected back to the Settings page. The card will now show <h>● Connected</h> along with the guild name.
discordBot.q1note: Only one Discord server can be linked per organization at a time.
discordBot.q2: How do I disconnect the Discord server?
discordBot.q2Body: In <h>Settings</h>, open the Discord Server card and click <c>Disconnect</c>. This removes the link. Slash commands and invite features will stop working until a new server is connected.
discordBot.q3: What slash commands does the bot provide?
discordBot.q3Intro: The bot provides three slash commands:
discordBot.q3SellBody: Create a <h>sell</h> request (member → org) without opening the web UI. The bot will ask for:
discordBot.q3SellItem: <c>item</c> — start typing to search your org's inventory (autocomplete, up to 25 results)
discordBot.q3BuyBody: Same as <c>/sell</c> but creates a <h>buy</h> request (org → member). Same fields.
discordBot.q3InventoryBody: Browse your org's current inventory directly from Discord. Shows all listed items with their buy price, sell price, and stock quantity.
discordBot.q3Outro: After submitting <c>/sell</c> or <c>/buy</c>, you will receive an ephemeral (private) confirmation. The transaction is created in Requested state and an embed is posted to the notification channel if one is configured.
discordBot.q4: What is the autocomplete for the item field?
discordBot.q4Body: When you start typing in the <c>item</c> field of <c>/sell</c> or <c>/buy</c>, Discord shows up to 25 matching items from your org's current inventory. The search is case-insensitive. Select one from the dropdown — you cannot type a free-form name.
discordBot.q5: What is the transaction notification embed?
discordBot.q5Intro: When a transaction is created (via web or Discord), the bot posts an embed to the configured notification channel. The embed shows:
discordBot.q5Li1: Item name, direction, quantity, price per unit, total, member username, optional note
discordBot.q6: What buttons appear on the Discord embed?
discordBot.q6Li1: — available when status is Requested. Moves the transaction to Approved.
discordBot.q6Li2: — available when status is Requested. Moves the transaction to Rejected.
discordBot.q6Li3: — available when status is Approved. Records that you completed the in-game exchange. Once both sides confirm, the transaction becomes Completed.
discordBot.q6Li4: — available when status is Requested or Approved. Moves the transaction to Cancelled.
discordBot.q6Outro: The embed is automatically updated in-place whenever the status changes, whether the action was taken on Discord or in the web UI.
discordBot.q7: Do Discord actions respect the same permission rules as the web UI?
discordBot.q7Body: Yes. The bot verifies your Discord account is linked to an org member account and that you have the correct role before allowing any action. The same business rules apply — you cannot approve your own request, confirm a trade for the other party, etc.

settingsSection.title: Settings
settingsSection.subtitle: Admin/owner configuration (admin/owner only)
settingsSection.q1: How do I set a Discord notification channel?
settingsSection.q1Step1: Make sure a Discord server is connected first (see above).
settingsSection.q1Step2: In <h>Settings</h>, scroll to the <h>Transaction Notifications</h> card.
settingsSection.q1Step3: Click the channel dropdown. It shows all text channels from your linked Discord server.
settingsSection.q1Step4: Select the channel where transaction embeds should be posted and click <c>Save Settings</c>.
settingsSection.q1Outro: To disable notifications, clear the selection and save. The <c>#</c> icon identifies text channels.
settingsSection.q2: What happens if no notification channel is set?
settingsSection.q2Body: Transactions created via the web UI will not post any Discord embed. Transactions created via <c>/sell</c> or <c>/buy</c> will still be saved and the user will get an ephemeral confirmation, but no channel embed will be posted.

auditLogs.title: Audit Logs
auditLogs.subtitle: Owner only — full activity history
auditLogs.q1: What is logged in the audit trail?
auditLogs.q1Intro: Every significant action is recorded with a timestamp, the acting user, and a description:
auditLogs.q1Li1: Transaction created, approved, rejected, confirmed, cancelled
auditLogs.q2: Who can see the audit logs?
auditLogs.q2Body: Only <h>owners</h>. The Logs page is hidden from admins and members in the navigation.

quickRef.title: Quick Reference
quickRef.subtitle: Slash commands at a glance
quickRef.sellWhat: Create a sell request (member → org) via Discord
quickRef.buyWhat: Create a buy request (org → member) via Discord
quickRef.inventoryWhat: Browse the org inventory from Discord
quickRef.noteText: To use slash commands or interact with embeds, your Discord account must be the one you used to log in to this terminal. The bot matches Discord user IDs to terminal accounts via the OAuth login.

billing.cancelError: Could not cancel subscription. Please try again.
billing.cancelDialogDesc: Your PRO access will continue until the end of the current billing period. After that, the organisation will revert to the Free plan.
billing.fetchError: Could not load billing data from Stripe. Please try again later.

reports.title: Reporting
reports.description: Weekly operation reports generated automatically every Monday. Download or generate reports for any past week.
reports.proRequiredDesc: Automated weekly reports are a PRO feature. Upgrade to access PDF reports, KPI summaries, and transaction history.
reports.generateConfirmMessage: A PDF report will be generated for the selected week. This may take a moment.
reports.alreadyExists: A report for this week already exists.
reports.autoNote: Reports are automatically generated every Monday at 02:00 UTC for the previous week. You can also generate or regenerate any week on demand.
reports.regenerateConfirmMessage: This will recalculate all KPIs and rebuild the PDF using current transaction data. The existing file will be overwritten.
reports.noReportsDesc: Click "Generate Report" to create your first weekly report, or wait for the Monday auto-generation.

cookieNotice.title: Cookie Notice
cookieNotice.body: SC Orga Manager uses essential cookies only — for login sessions, CSRF protection, and your language preference. No tracking, no analytics, no third-party advertising cookies.
